data.title: Guess? Q4 results top expectations, sets mixed guidance
data.description:
Driven by its rag & bone acquisition and positive momentum in the company’s European and American wholesale business, Guess? (NYSE:GES) reported better-than-expected quarterly results and announced plans to optimize its portfolio to unlock approximately $30M in operating profit in FY27.

Shares were launched higher on the Q4 beat, but reversed gains on mixed guidance that includes a wider-than-expected loss for the current quarter.

With growth in all its segments, except Asia, the company’s total revenue increased by 4.6% in the fourth quarter to an above consensus $932.25M, while a profit of $1.48 per share was 10 cents better than anticipated.

Due to slower traffic in North America and Asia, the company missed its expectations for its direct-to-consumer business but will focus its strategic initiatives this year on increasing DTC sales productivity globally and improving profitability through business and portfolio optimization.

In connection with this, Guess? (NYSE:GES) will look for a partner to run its direct operations in Greater China, streamline the Guess? (NYSE:GES) full price store portfolio by exiting non-strategic, unprofitable locations, and reduce costs by consolidating the infrastructure supporting this business. The company’s FY26 outlook includes the anticipated impact from these actions.

For FY26, the company expects revenue to increase by 3.9% to 6.2%, representing a range of $3.117B to $3.186B, above the consensus estimate of $3.04B. Adjusted earnings are expected to be between $1.32 to $1.76 per share, below the $2.02 estimate.

For Q1, Guess? (GES) expects revenue to increase by 5.8% to 7.5% from Q1 last year, representing a range of $627.3M to $636.4M, which straddles the consensus estimate of $629.2M. The company also expects to report a loss of $(0.74) to $(0.65) per share, wider than the estimated loss of $(0.23).

data.title: RiverNorth Capital and Income Fund announces final results of repurchase offer
data.description:
• RiverNorth Capital and Income Fund (NYSE:RSF) announced the final results of its repurchase offer for up to 5% of its outstanding common shares.
  • The repurchase offer expired at 5:00 P.M. Eastern Time on April 2, 2025.
  • Based on information provided by DST Systems, the depositary for the repurchase offer, a total of 1.54M shares were submitted for redemption and 174,273 shares were repurchased.
  • In accordance with the terms and conditions of the repurchase offer, because the number of shares submitted for redemption exceeds the number of shares offered to purchase, the Fund will purchase shares from tendering shareholders on a pro-rata basis.
  • The purchase price of repurchased shares is equal to the Fund's net asset value per share calculated as of the close of regular trading on the NYSE on April 2, 2025, which is equal to $16.15 per share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cboe multiply-listed options ADV roughly flat M/M in March, as EU outpaces North America
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) multiply-listed options average daily volume ("ADV") inched down 0.2% M/M to 13.5M contracts in March, though it climbed 28% from a year earlier, the derivatives exchange said on Thursday.

The stagnant March reading came as robust trading in Europe was offset by a lackluster performance across the U.S. and Canada.

U.S. equities - on exchange - ADV fell 3.4% sequentially and 10.5% Y/Y to 1.62B matched shares. 

Canadian equities ADV of 154.0M slid 7.4% from a month before and 3.6% from last year's March.

European equities ADV of €16.4B ($18.1B) vs. €13.7B in February and €10.3B in March 2024.

Meanwhile, index options ADV of 5.27M contracts advanced from 4.47M in February and 4.06M in the year-ago period.

Futures ADV totaled 285K contracts, up from 241K in the prior month and 217K in March 2024.

Global foreign exchange ADV was $54.8B, up from $50.7B in the previous month and $47.4B in March 2024.

data.title: Ryan Cohen adds to his GameStop stake with a purchase of 500K shares
data.description:
GameStop (NYSE:GME) moved higher in postmarket trading after the retailer released an SEC filing indicating that CEO Ryan Cohen purchased 500,000 shares in the open market during the day. Cohen picked up the shares at a price of $21.55.

Cohen holds more than 37 million shares of GameStop, which is a stake of about 8.4% of the company.

Last week, the company announced its board unanimously approved an update to its investment policy to add Bitcoin. Notably, GameStop (NYSE:GME) ended Q4 with $4.76 billion in cash that can be deployed. However, shares fell sharply the next day when GameStop (GME) announced plans for a private offering of $1.3 billion in convertible senior notes. Those funds will be used for proceeds for general corporate purposes, which could include investing in Bitcoin.

GameStop (GME) closed down 7.0% for the day at $21.10 vs. the 52-week trading range of $9.95 to $64.83. In the postmarket session, GameStop (GME) tracked 2.2% higher. Short interest on GME is 6.6% of the total float.

data.title: ReTo regains compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule
data.description:
• ReTo Eco-Solutions (NASDAQ:RETO) received a letter from Nasdaq on March 28 notifying that it has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The letter stated that the company will remain on a discretionary panel monitor for a one-year period from the date of the letter.
  • RETO -2.70% after hours to $3.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amgen got expanded FDA label approval for Uplizna
data.description:
Amgen (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:AMGN) announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted an expanded label approval for Uplizna, now approved as a treatment for immunoglobulin G4-related disease ((IgG4-RD)) in adults.

IgG4-RD is a chronic immune system disorder that causes inflammation and fibrosis in multiple organs, often leading to organ dysfunction over time. UPLIZNA is also indicated for the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder, a rare autoimmune disease of the central nervous system.

The expanded indication for IgG4-RD is based on results from a late-stage clinical trial, which showed that Uplizna reduced the risk of disease flare by 87% compared to a placebo.

data.title: WW International investor launches proxy fight for board seats: report
data.description:
Premca Capital, which has a small stake in Weight Watchers parent company WW International (NASDAQ:WW) is seeking seats on its board as well as cost cuts totalling hundreds of millions of dollars, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

According to the report, the investment fund, which has a stake of less than 1% in WW, has nominated three directors for the company’s board, the people said. 

The people were quoted as saying that Premca has been in talks with WW since November. 

WW’s share price has plummeted around 70% over the past year as it has shifted more of its efforts toward GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, such as Wegovy and Ozempic. 

The people reportedly said Premca wants to slash WW’s costs by $200M to $300M, along with the cuts that it has already been undertaking. They added that Premca also wants to reset WW’s management, improve Weight Watchers’ app, and adopt a plan for growth, the report said. 

Premca Chief Investment Officer Rajit Marwah told Seeking Alpha that the firm wants WW to return to a $1B market capitalization after the 95% drop in the company’s share price under its current board. 

WW did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment.
